additionalInstruction: Round 4 targeted unblock attempt. The app is already instrumented with stable selectors: input[data-testid='image-upload-input'], [data-testid='palette-loading'], [data-testid='source-image'], [data-testid='palette-color-0'], [data-testid='format-hex'], [data-testid='format-rgb'], [data-testid='format-hsl'], [data-testid='copy-all-colors'], [data-testid='export-css'], [data-testid='export-json'], [data-testid='export-tailwind'], [data-testid='primary-palette-cta'], and [data-testid='secondary-palette-cta']. IMPORTANT: do not write upload fixtures to /tmp and do not depend on repo paths or available_file_paths. Use Playwright Python in-memory file payloads with page.locator("input[data-testid='image-upload-input']").set_input_files(files=[{"name":"palette.png","mimeType":"image/png","buffer": <bytes>}]) or the Python equivalent accepted by Playwright. Build the bytes in memory from base64.b64decode for valid PNGs, b'not a png' for corrupted.png, b'not an image' for invalid text, and bytes large enough to exceed 10MB for the oversized image. This should exercise upload without filesystem access. Re-run all 15 tests with priority on upload success, palette extraction, HEX/RGB/HSL switching, copy single color, copy all, CSS/JSON/Tailwind export, invalid file rejection, corrupted image error text, oversized file rejection, tiny image handling, drag/drop if feasible, mobile viewport, and both CTA navigation tests. Expected corrupted image error text: The image could not be processed. Please try another image.
